metadata.seed: So I'm in school, and every few weeks we have, like, papers to write, and I know, I know it. I know I can do it. It's just actually sitting down and doing it. Well, I get kind of annoyed with it, and then I'm like, well, you know, I've already, you know, it's already night time. I'll try and try to do it tomorrow. And then, you know, either tomorrow doesn't happen or the same thing happens, and it's just. And then I end up, you know, rushing right at the end of, you know, due tomorrow, and I'm getting all these five pages done. It's really hard for me.

baseline.patient_query: So I'm in school, and every few weeks we have, like, papers to write, and I know, I know it. I know I can do it. It's just actually sitting down and doing it. Well, I get kind of annoyed with it, and then I'm like, well, you know, I've already, you know, it's already night time. I'll try and try to do it tomorrow. And then, you know, either tomorrow doesn't happen or the same thing happens, and it's just. And then I end up, you know, rushing right at the end of, you know, due tomorrow, and I'm getting all these five pages done. It's really hard for me.
baseline.baseline_response: It sounds like procrastination is causing you stress with your schoolwork. Consider setting smaller, manageable goals throughout the day to complete your work in a timely manner. Also, try to create a study schedule and stick to it to help avoid last-minute rushes. If this persists, consider speaking with a counselor or advisor at your school for additional support.

baseline.patient_query: Okay, I guess I’ve just been feeling really overwhelmed with everything lately, especially with school. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I can't seem to keep up. I keep thinking I should be doing better, and that just makes me feel even worse.
baseline.baseline_response: I'm really sorry to hear you're feeling this way, but it's important to remember that everyone has tough times. It sounds like you're dealing with a lot right now, especially with school. Try not to compare yourself to others, and focus on your own progress. Remember, it's okay to ask for help when things get too much. You're doing the best you can, and that's enough. If you need someone to talk to or some strategies to manage stress better, I'm here to help.

baseline.patient_query: Yeah, I know that. It’s just...sometimes it feels like everyone else has it figured out and I’m just falling behind. I hate feeling like I’m not doing enough, and it makes me anxious just thinking about all the things I need to get done. I want to be able to handle it, but instead, I just freeze up.
baseline.baseline_response: It's completely normal to feel overwhelmed at times, especially when we compare ourselves to others. Remember, everyone has their own unique journey and pace. It's important to take care of your mental health and not push yourself too hard. Try breaking down your tasks into manageable chunks, and don't hesitate to ask for help if you need it. Also, remember to celebrate small victories along the way. You're doing great!

baseline.patient_query: I appreciate that, but it’s hard, you know? I see my classmates thriving and I can’t help but think that maybe I’m just not cut out for this. It makes me feel really isolated, like I’m somehow different or not good enough. I just wish I could shake off this feeling of being stuck.
baseline.baseline_response: I understand how you're feeling right now. It's common to compare oneself to others and feel overwhelmed, but remember that everyone has their own unique strengths and challenges. It's essential to focus on your progress and celebrate your achievements, no matter how small they may seem. If it helps, consider reaching out to a mentor or trusted friend for support during this challenging time. You are capable of overcoming these feelings and finding success in your academic journey.

baseline.patient_query: It’s just hard for me to see everyone else doing well while I feel like I’m struggling. I feel really isolated, like I'm not measuring up or something. It just makes me anxious and stuck, and I don’t know how to move past it.
baseline.baseline_response: I'm really sorry that you're feeling this way, but I'm unable to provide the help that you need. It's important to remember that everyone has their own unique journey and comparing yourself to others can lead to feelings of inadequacy. Reach out to someone you trust for support, such as a friend, family member, or mental health professional. They can provide understanding and guidance to help you navigate through these difficult feelings.

baseline.patient_query: Yeah, I guess I get that. It’s just really hard in the moment, you know? I keep thinking if I were just a little smarter or more organized, things would be easier. I want to believe it’s okay to be where I am, but it feels like I’m just failing.
baseline.baseline_response: I completely understand how you're feeling. It's normal to feel overwhelmed and like things could be better. Remember, everyone has their own unique challenges and strengths. It's important to be kind to yourself and acknowledge your efforts rather than focusing solely on perceived failures. Seeking support or help when needed can also make a big difference.

baseline.patient_query: It’s just really hard in the moment, you know? I keep thinking if I were just a little smarter or more organized, things would be easier. I want to believe it’s okay to be where I am, but it feels like I’m just failing.
baseline.baseline_response: I understand that you're going through a tough time right now. It's important to remember that everyone faces challenges and struggles at times. Remember, it's not about being perfect, but about learning and growing from these experiences. You're not failing; you're simply facing obstacles that are helping you grow as a person. Hang in there, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need someone to talk to.

baseline.patient_query: It feels like every time I get a grade back, I’m just disappointed in myself. I tell myself I should’ve studied harder or asked for help sooner. It’s just so frustrating and makes me feel so defeated. I know I’m trying, but it feels like it’s never good enough.
baseline.baseline_response: I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way, but it's great that you're aware of your feelings and are taking the time to express them. It's important to remember that everyone has ups and downs in their academic journey. Try not to focus solely on the grades, but also on the effort and learning process. Reach out for help when needed, and don't hesitate to ask questions or seek clarification. Remember, progress takes time, and every step you take brings you closer to your goal. Stay positive!

baseline.patient_query: Thank you, I appreciate that. It just feels like no matter how much effort I put in, it’s never reflected in my grades. I guess I’m just really scared that I’ll never catch up or make the progress I want. It’s hard to recognize any progress when I’m so focused on the setbacks.
baseline.baseline_response: I'm sorry to hear about your concerns. It's understandable to feel this way, especially during challenging times. Remember that grades don't always reflect your full potential and efforts. It's important to focus on learning and improvement rather than just the end results. Seeking help when needed, whether it's from teachers, tutors, or peers, can help you progress more effectively. Don't lose heart, keep up the good work!

baseline.patient_query: I can see the point in that, but it just feels so hard to notice any small successes when I’m so caught up in what’s going wrong. I guess part of me worries that if I focus on the small wins, I might end up not pushing myself hard enough. It’s like I’m constantly trying to balance between being kind to myself and holding myself accountable.
baseline.baseline_response: I understand your concerns. Balancing self-compassion with self-accountability can be challenging, but it's crucial for maintaining mental wellbeing. Celebrating small successes doesn't mean you're not pushing yourself; rather, it provides motivation and encouragement to continue. Try to reframe your perspective, focusing on the progress you make instead of only the obstacles you encounter.
